$comment: Registry of protected-path candidates init suggests. Adding a new candidate is a JSON-only PR — no engine code change required. Each `conditional` entry has a `detect` predicate (devDependency / fileExists / dirExists) and emits its `paths` + `rationale` when the predicate matches. Order of entries determines the order in the generated config; init dedupes by path so an entry that already matched an `always` rule is not re-emitted.

always.rationale: The constitution itself. Workers must not edit the rules they are being held to.

conditional.rationale: ESLint config controls lint behavior; editing it changes what `verify` enforces.
Biome config controls lint and format behavior.
Prettier config controls formatting; editing it shifts what passes format-check.
CI workflows are the deployment gate; editing them changes what runs.
Pre-commit hooks enforce policy before code lands.

Scripts and dependency declarations are constitutional; editing them changes what tools run. Workers legitimately needing new dependencies should surface that via kickBack rather than editing this file directly.
